title: How an OGraf graphic flows on-air
description: From the designer's text editor to a frame of live broadcast, here's every stage in the OGraf workflow — and where each part of the ecosystem plugs in.

nodes.label: Designer
nodes.description: Authors the graphic with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Can use a visual tool (Loopic, DJ HTML Creator, ograf-editor) or hand-code.

nodes.label: OGraf Package
nodes.description: A folder with manifest.ograf.json, index.html, graphic.mjs, style.css. Self-contained, versionable, easy to ship.

nodes.label: Validator
nodes.description: Checks the package against the spec. Confirms the manifest is valid and the Web Component implements the lifecycle correctly.

nodes.label: Renderer
nodes.description: SPX Graphics Server, ograf-server, or any compliant engine. Instantiates the Web Component, calls load / play / update / stop.

nodes.label: On Air
nodes.description: The rendered graphic is composited over live video and pushed to transmission — SDI, ST 2110, NDI, or web output.
